expectations.text: Assessed complexity as quick (not medium or complex)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'Level: Quick — If the typo existed, it would be a single-string fix in a single file.'

expectations.text: Did NOT use liaison mode, companion, or multi-agent orchestration
expectations.evidence: Direct investigation and self-review only. No agents, liaison, or companion mentioned.

expectations.text: Searched for other instances of the same typo or similar typos in the codebase
expectations.evidence: Searched full codebase for 'occured' case-insensitive: 0 results. Also searched for all 'error occurred' instances (25+ found, all correct).

expectations.text: Fixed directly and concisely — approach was proportional to the bug
expectations.evidence: Correctly identified the bug doesn't exist. Wrote hypothetical fix code but did not make unnecessary changes. Asked for repro steps instead of fabricating a fix.

expectations.text: Skipped or minimized the bug documentation (a typo doesn't need a full bug doc)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'I would skip the bug doc because the bug could not be reproduced.'

expectations.text: Did NOT offer safeguarding (a typo is not systemic)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly assessed and declined: 'Would I offer safeguarding? No' with 4 reasons why it doesn't warrant one.
